response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is built around web and full-stack development using TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His only identifiable C++ work is a roughly six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project, neither of which constitutes the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development the role requires. He has no discernible experience in fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets systems, and his SQL exposure appears incidental rather than a demonstrated strength in SQL Server or Oracle. The role's mandatory requirements are almost entirely unmet.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience across multiple employers, satisfying one desirable criterion
Java background (Altom Consulting, robotic arms project) partially satisfies the optional Java desirable skill
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing record through public talks, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows
Zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any financial-domain systems
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; primary database experience is MongoDB and general SQL
